_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The ICAO Global Framework does not itself impose a direct registration requirement on freight forwarders. Registration and reporting obligations under the framework fall into two tiers:

**Tier 1 — Aircraft Operators (direct CORSIA obligations):** Under Annex 16, Volume IV, aeroplane operators on international routes between ICAO Member States must monitor CEF claims (§2.2.4), report CEF use in annual emissions reports (§2.3.3), and have those claims verified (§2.4.3). These obligations are imposed on airlines, not on forwarders. The workspace's airline partners on Americas/Europe/Asia routes will carry these obligations and are expected to begin mandatory reporting for the Second Phase from 2027.

**Tier 2 — Fuel Producers/Economic Operators (CORSIA SCS certification):** Fuel producers seeking CORSIA SAF or LCAF designation must obtain certification from an ICAO Council-approved Sustainability Certification Scheme. The CORSIA Approved SCS document (3rd Edition, October 2024) identifies approved schemes. The CORSIA Eligibility Framework (4th Edition, June 2025) sets requirements for those schemes. Fuel batches must carry chain-of-custody documentation traceable through the value chain.

**Tier 3 — Intermediaries and Freight Forwarders (voluntary/market-driven):** The IATA SAF Registry (launched by CADO in April 2025 and upgraded September 2025) provides dedicated account types for freight forwarders and intermediaries. Registration on the SAF Registry is not mandated by ICAO but is rapidly becoming a market expectation for any workspace offering SAF-attributed emission reductions to cargo clients. Registration enables the workspace to receive SAF environmental attribute certificates from airlines, attribute them to specific cargo shipments, and pass them to clients for Scope 3 reporting. As of April 2026, the Registry hosts over 130 organisations.

**Data fields the workspace must collect for SAF attribution and client reporting:**
- Shipment-level fuel quantity and flight routing data (from airline e-AWB or flight manifest)
- CORSIA Eligible Fuel batch certification number and approved SCS name
- Lifecycle GHG emissions value (default or actual) for the SAF batch used
- Volume of SAF attributed to cargo consignment (by weight or volume allocation method)
- Book-and-claim certificate reference number (if not physical uplift)
- Destination jurisdiction SAF mandate compliance status (EU, UK, US, Singapore, India, Japan)

**Published vs. Promised:** The ICAO CORSIA MRV standards under Annex 16 Volume IV are published and in force. The IATA SAF Registry interoperability with State compliance programmes is described as a forward roadmap item, not yet released as a formal standard. The ICAO Finvest Hub for financing SAF projects is operationalised as a facilitation mechanism but formal financing instruments under it are not yet published.

*Sources: ICAO CORSIA Eligible Fuels page, ICAO, Nov 2025. https://www.icao.int/CORSIA/corsia-eligible-fuels; IATA SAF Registry/CADO, IATA, April 2026. https://airlines.iata.org/2026/04/14/ensuring-transparency-saf-environmental-benefits*
